title: Sarga 81
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “After a short while, the divine sage of immeasurable splendor, surrounded by his disciples and afflicted by hunger, returned to his hermitage, having heard (something amiss).” and develops toward “Having heard the words of Ushanas (Shukra), the people dwelling in that hermitage then departed from that region and established their abode outside.”

This passage begins with “Having thus spoken to the sages, he then said to Araja, "O foolish one, dwell here in this hermitage, well-composed."” and develops toward “O Rama, the sun has set, having been worshipped by those best knowers of Brahman who recite the Vedas; therefore, go and perform your ablutions.…”

verses.sanskrit: स मुहूर्तादुपश्रुत्य देवर्षिरमितप्रभः । स्वमाश्रमं शिष्यवृतः क्षुधार्तः सन्न्यवर्तत ।। 7.81.1 ।।
verses.translation: After a short while, the divine sage of immeasurable splendor, surrounded by his disciples and afflicted by hunger, returned to his hermitage, having heard (something amiss).

verses.sanskrit: सो ऽपश्यदरजां दीनां रजसा समभिप्लुताम् । ज्योत्स्नामिव ग्रहग्रस्तां प्रत्यूषे न विराजतीम् ।। 7.81.2 ।।
verses.translation: He saw Arajā, distressed and covered with impurity, like moonlight eclipsed by a planet and not shining brightly at dawn.

verses.sanskrit: तस्य रोषः समभवत्क्षुधार्तस्य विशेषतः । निर्दहन्निव लोकांस्त्रीञ्छिष्यांश्चैतदुवाच ह ।। 7.81.3 ।।
verses.translation: His anger, especially due to hunger, arose as if burning the three worlds, and he spoke this to his disciples.

verses.sanskrit: पश्यध्वं विपरीतस्य दण्डस्याविजितात्मनः । विपत्तिं घोरसङ्काशां क्रुद्धामग्निशिखामिव ।। 7.81.4 ।।
verses.translation: Behold the terrible calamity, like an enraged flame of fire, that will befall the perverse and uncontrolled King Daṇḍa.

verses.sanskrit: क्षयो ऽस्य दुर्मतेः प्राप्तः सानुगस्य दुरात्मनः । यः प्रदीप्तां हुताशस्य शिखां वै स्प्रष्टुमिच्छति ।। 7.81.5 ।।
verses.translation: The destruction of this evil-minded and wicked one, along with his followers, is imminent, for he desires to touch a blazing flame of fire.

verses.sanskrit: यस्मात्स कृतवान्पापमीदृशं घोरसंहितम् । तस्मात्प्राप्स्यति दुर्मेधाः फलं पापस्य कर्मणः ।। 7.81.6 ।।
verses.translation: Because he has committed such a terrible sin, that foolish one will therefore receive the consequence of his evil deed.

verses.sanskrit: सप्तरात्रेण राजासौ सभृत्यबलवाहनः । पापकर्मसमाचारो वधं प्राप्स्यति दुर्मतिः ।। 7.81.7 ।।
verses.translation: Within seven nights, that evil-minded king, whose conduct is sinful, will meet his death along with his servants, army, and vehicles.

verses.sanskrit: समन्ताद्योजनशतं विषयं चास्य दुर्मतेः । धक्ष्यते पांसुवर्षेण महता पाकशासनः ।। 7.81.8 ।।
verses.translation: Shukra declares that Indra will destroy the evil-minded Daṇḍa's region, extending a hundred yojanas in all directions, with a mighty shower of dust.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वसत्वानि यानीह स्थावराणि चराणि च । महता पांसुवर्षेण विलयं सर्वतो ऽगमन् ।। 7.81.9 ।।
verses.translation: All beings, both mobile and immobile, present in that region, completely met their destruction due to the great shower of dust.

verses.sanskrit: दण्डस्य विषयो यावत्तावत्सर्वसमुच्छ्रयम् । पांसुवर्षमिवालक्ष्यं सप्तरात्रं भविष्यति ।। 7.81.10 ।।
verses.translation: For seven nights, the entire region of Daṇḍa, including all elevated structures, will become imperceptible, as if covered by a continuous shower of dust.

verses.sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा क्रोधताम्राक्षस्तदाश्रमनिवासिनम् । जनं जनपदान्तेषु स्थीयतामिति चाब्रवीत् ।। 7.81.11 ।।
verses.translation: Having spoken thus, Shukra, whose eyes were red with anger, instructed the people residing in that hermitage to stay at the borders of the country.

verses.sanskrit: श्रुत्वा तूशनसो वाक्यं सो ऽ ऽश्रमावसथो जनः । निष्क्रान्तो विषयात्तस्मात्स्थानं चक्रे ऽथ बाह्यतः ।। 7.81.12 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard the words of Ushanas (Shukra), the people dwelling in that hermitage then departed from that region and established their abode outside.

verses.sanskrit: स तथोक्त्वा मुनिजनमरजामिदमब्रवीत् । इहैव वस दुर्मेधे आश्रमे सुसमाहिता ।। 7.81.13 ।।
verses.translation: Having thus spoken to the sages, he then said to Araja, "O foolish one, dwell here in this hermitage, well-composed."

verses.sanskrit: इदं योजनपर्यन्तं सरः सुरुचिरप्रभम् । अरजे विज्वरा भुङ्क्ष्व कालश्चात्र प्रतीक्ष्यताम् ।। 7.81.14 ।।
verses.translation: O Araja, enjoy this lake, which extends for a yojana and possesses a very beautiful splendor, free from distress, and let time pass here.

verses.sanskrit: त्वत्समीपे च ये सत्त्वा वासमेष्यन्ति तां निशाम् । अवध्याः पांसुवर्षेण ते भविष्यन्ति नित्यदा ।। 7.81.15 ।।
verses.translation: And those beings who come to dwell near you for that night will always be invulnerable to the shower of dust.

verses.sanskrit: श्रुत्वा नियोगं ब्रह्मर्षेः सा ऽरजा भार्गवी तदा । तथेति पितरं प्राह भार्गवं भृशदुःखिता । इत्युक्त्वा भार्गवो वासमन्यत्र समकारयत् ।। 7.81.16 ।।
verses.translation: Greatly distressed, Araja, the daughter of Bhargava, then said "So be it" to her father, the Brahmarshi, after hearing his command; having said this, Bhargava then arranged for his dwelling elsewhere.

verses.sanskrit: तच्च राज्यं नरेन्द्रस्य सभृत्यबलवाहनम् । सप्ताहाद्भस्मसाद्भूतं यथोक्तं ब्रह्मवादिना ।। 7.81.17 ।।
verses.translation: And that kingdom of the king, along with its servants, army, and vehicles, was reduced to ashes within seven days, just as the sage had foretold.

verses.sanskrit: तस्यासौ दण्डविषयो विन्ध्यशैवलयोर्नृप । शप्तो ब्रह्मर्षिणा तेन वैधर्म्ये सहिते कृते ।। 7.81.18 ।।
verses.translation: O king, this region of Daṇḍa, situated between the Vindhya and Śaivala mountains, was cursed by that Brahmarṣi when unrighteousness was committed there.

verses.sanskrit: ततः प्रभृति काकुत्स्थ दण्डकारण्यमुच्यते । तपस्विनः स्थिता ह्यत्र जनस्थानमतो ऽभवत् ।। 7.81.19 ।।
verses.translation: O Kakutstha, from that time onwards, this region is called Daṇḍakāraṇya, and because ascetics resided here, it also became known as Janasthāna.

verses.sanskrit: एतत्ते सर्वमाख्यातं यन्मां पृच्छसि राघव । सन्ध्यामुपासितुं वीर समयो ह्यतिवर्तते ।। 7.81.20 ।।
verses.translation: O Raghava, I have told you all that you asked me, but O hero, the time for performing the twilight prayer is indeed passing.

verses.sanskrit: एते महर्षयः सर्वे पूर्णकुम्भाः समन्ततः । कृतोदका नरव्याघ्र आदित्यं पर्युपासते ।। 7.81.21 ।।
verses.translation: O tiger among men, all these great sages, with their water pots full and having performed their water rituals, are worshipping the sun god all around.

verses.sanskrit: स तैर्ब्राह्मणमभ्यस्तं सहितैर्ब्रह्मवित्तमैः । रविरस्तं गतो राम गच्छोदकमुपस्पृश ।। 7.81.22 ।।
verses.translation: O Rama, the sun has set, having been worshipped by those best knowers of Brahman who recite the Vedas; therefore, go and perform your ablutions. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे एकाशीतितमः सर्गः ।। 81 ।। Thus ends the eighty-first sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
